Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Select the correct statements with reference to a radio receiver:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
• Sensitivity is the ability of a receiver to detect the weakest possible signal.
• Selectivity is the receiver’s ability to distinguish between two adjacent carrier frequencies.
• Fidelity is the ability of a receiver to reproduce faithfully all frequency components present in
the baseband signal.
• Double spotting in a radio receiver refers to the phenomenon when signals from the same
short waves station is picked at two nearby points on the receiver tuning dial.

Q20 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A carrier signal is amplitude modulated by a sinusoidal message signal m1(t) with modulation index of 0.5. When another sinusoidal message signal m2(t) is added to m1(t), without changing any other parameter, the antenna current of the AM transmitter is increased by 8%. If the same
AM transmitter with same carrier signal is excited with m2(t) alone, the transmission efficiency of the resultant AM signal is _________%. [Rounded off to one decimal place]

NAT
Correct answer: 15.8
Solution

15.8 (15.5 - 16.0)

While increasing Am from 0 volts, it is found that the carrier component of the FM signal is reduced to zero for the first time when Am =  volts. The value of Am for which the carrier component is reduced to zero for the third time is _______ volts. (correct to three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 5.375
Solution

5.375 (5.000 - 6.000)

NAT
Correct answer: 5.20
Solution

5.20 (5.00 - 5.50)
